narrative: Your undergraduate GPA of 2.60 stood out as the primary reason the model predicted you'd be likely to struggle with the bar exam, as it falls notably below the 3.24 average among students who passed on their first try, strongly pushing the prediction toward failure. Alongside that, your upper-middle family income bracket, while common among successful students at 45%, appears to have added some concern in the model's assessment, perhaps signaling patterns it associates with other risks not captured here. Your Year 1 grade decile of 7 also contributed toward the failure prediction, even though it's slightly above the 6.04 average for passers, suggesting the model weighed it as a moderate drag in your overall profile. These three factors—your undergrad grades, family background, and early academic ranking—were the most influential in tipping the scales, far outweighing positives like your solid LSAT score of 40, which is above the 37.27 average for those who passed. Overall, the model estimated a 72% chance you'd fail the bar exam on the first attempt, leading to the rejection of your law school application to protect the program's outcomes. Compared to typical admitted students, who often have stronger undergrad records and smoother early trajectories, your profile aligned more closely with patterns the model links to bar struggles. Your full-time study plan fits the norm for passers, who are 93% full-time, but it couldn't offset the top concerns. This isn't a judgment on your potential—models like this look at historical patterns, not your full story or drive. To move forward, consider retaking undergrad courses or pursuing a post-baccalaureate program to boost your GPA, as even small improvements there could shift future predictions dramatically. You might also gather letters highlighting your resilience or LSAT strength to supplement applications elsewhere. Many students in similar spots succeed by targeting schools with holistic reviews or bar prep support. Keep pushing; your LSAT shows you have the aptitude to turn this around.
